title: Hands on the Hurt
description: Harmonia, ch.1. A villager has been hurt. The Cleric steps forward without being asked.

steps.text: An old woman sat on the edge of the well, holding her shin. A scrape — not deep, but the wrong shape of dark, and the kind of fall that doesn't stop being a fall when you stand up.

cleric.text: The lead was already there. They had been there before anyone noticed the woman was hurt.
Their hands were steady. The cut was closed before the woman finished saying she was fine. This is what the lead does. This is what the lead has always done.

default.text: She didn't ask permission. She didn't introduce herself. She knelt — her sandals were impractical for kneeling — and put her hands on the woman's shin and closed her eyes.
For half a second she paused. The kind of half-second nobody would have noticed if they hadn't been looking at her hands. Then the prayer came. The bleeding stopped. The woman's breath caught.
The cut was not gone — healing didn't make wounds disappear, the Cleric would later explain, it simply taught them to be over with — but it was closed.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Walk on it tomorrow. Today, sit.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Tell me if it hurts in the night. I'll come back.

default.text: She meant it. She would come back. The Cleric was, the party would learn, the kind of person who said things and then did them.
